basics.name: Minkyeong Cadiz
basics.label: Student
basics.summary: I am studying for a B.A. in Information Computer Science in the Department of Information and Computer Sciences at the University of Hawaii. I expect to graduate in Spring, 2026.

highlights: Managed and updated the Student Athlete Academic Services website using WordPress.
Provided troubleshooting and technical support for students and staff.
Collaborated with academic advisors to track student progress and ensure ongoing support tailored to each student's needs.
Modernized slit mask design software for the MOIRCS instrument by migrating from IDL to a Python based system.
Enhanced functionality and performance by integrating with Ginga.

Reasearch.title: Building a UV Fluorescence Modeling Pipeline for Young Planetary Systems
highlights: Refactored 6 Jupyter notebooks into a structured Python package to build a reusable UV fluorescence modeling piple line for young planetary systems.
Pipeline is reusable for any star with CO gas emission — thousands of targets exist in SPHEREx and SPLICE catalogss.

Reasearch.title: Soil Data Extraction
highlights: Developed an LLM pipeline to extract structured soil data from scientific datasets.
Built evaluation tools including an accuracy matrix for model performance analysis.
Assisted children in learning to play musical instruments, including piano and flute, and in reading and understanding musical notation.

awards.title: Merit Scholarship for Non US Citizen Students
awards.summary: Awarded to international students whose citizenship is outside of the U.S. and who require an F-1 Visa to enroll at UH, with a cumulative 3.3 college GPA or higher will be considered for this scholarship award.
